title: quercus glauca var. lacera
intro: Quercus glauca var. lacera, also known as the Japanese evergreen oak, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its elliptical leaves and small acorns. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese and Japanese herbalism, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus glauca var. lacera are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin infections.
